title: Live worker activity painter
goal: Make Herdr worker panes visibly useful during long Pi worker attempts by painting the daemon-owned wrapper stdout event stream live, without changing the correctness path or making terminal scrollback authoritative.
acceptance: A read-only worker activity painter can follow a Herdr wrapper stdout.ndjson artifact with tail-F semantics and render live Pi worker activity in the worker pane.
All Pi event interpretation for the painter goes through src/pi_contract.rs; no new module parses Pi wire-format event names or fields directly.
High-volume message_update/token deltas are throttled or compacted for human display without dropping the underlying daemon-owned artifact data.
The worker correctness path is unchanged: Khazad-Doom still reads wrapper stdout/stderr/status/exit/result artifacts, never Herdr pane text, terminal scrollback, or Herdr agent-status metadata.
The painter is not an authority channel: it accepts no worker instructions, answers, approvals, or manual result JSON, and operator actions still go through daemon commands such as answer or cancel.

If the painter process crashes or is killed while the Pi worker continues, the worker attempt completes, fails, or is cancelled according to the same daemon-owned wrapper/result path as before; the failure is at most a non-fatal cockpit visibility incident.

A bounded recorded event-stream fixture derived from HERDR/RPL dogfood artifacts is promoted into tests and covers message, tool, turn, unknown-field, and unknown-event tolerance for the painter/contract path.
must_ask_if: The implementation would parse Herdr pane scrollback, terminal text, or Herdr agent-status metadata as correctness evidence.
The implementation would make the painter required for worker completion, verification, merge, handoff, or result capture.
The implementation would add a second Pi event parser outside src/pi_contract.rs.
The implementation would let operators type answers, approvals, or worker result JSON into the worker pane as accepted workflow input.
The implementation would weaken the HERDR-02 wrapper artifact contract or remove daemon-owned stdout/stderr/exit/result files.
